children.text: in the absence of the Secretary, serve as the Secretary’s representative for interagency and multilateral policy discussions of global climate change, including the activities of the Committee on Earth and Environmental Sciences as established by the Global Change Research Act of 1990 () [ et seq.] and the Policy Coordinating Committee Working Group on Climate Change;Public Law 101–60615 U.S.C. 2921
monitor, in cooperation with other Federal agencies, domestic and international policies for their effects on the generation of greenhouse gases; and
have the authority to participate in the planning activities of relevant Department of Energy programs.
